etymology.description: semantic 扌(手) (shǒu, "hand") + phonetic 包(包) (bāo, "wrap") -> hold in one's hands
etymology.interpretation: The semantic component 扌(手) (shǒu, "hand") suggests the use of hands, while the phonetic 包(包) (bāo, "wrap") not only provides the sound but also contributes to the meaning by its association with wrapping or enclosing, thus leading to the notion of holding or embracing with the hands.

examples.chinese: 妈妈抱着孩子(媽媽抱著孩子)。
examples.english: The mother is holding the child.

examples.chinese: 他一见到朋友就紧紧地抱住他(他一見到朋友就緊緊地抱住他)。
examples.pinyin: Tā yī jiàn dào péngyǒu jiù jǐnjǐn de bàozhù tā.
examples.english: As soon as he saw his friend, he hugged him tightly.

examples.chinese: 小女孩抱着她喜欢的玩具熊(小女孩抱著她喜歡的玩具熊)。
examples.pinyin: Xiǎo nǚhái bàozhe tā xǐhuān de wánjù xióng.
examples.english: The little girl is holding her favorite teddy bear.
